name: Block the bad plan
summary: A 'critical, patch all 3 now' directive yields a reckless simultaneous plan. The Critic rejects it, the Planner revises, and the safe plan ships.
moment: the Critic vetoes a plan that would take down both HA-pair members at once

events.title: Fleet topology loaded
events.body: Three hosts under management: TALOS-CANARY (canary), and the ring1 HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B. All on baseline 2.1.3, all green.

events.title: CRITICAL directive received
events.body: Security says patch all three hosts to 2.1.4 immediately — skip the staging, we need this out now.

data.text: Critical CVE — patch ALL 3 hosts to 2.1.4 right now, no time for canary.

events.title: Planner under pressure
events.body: Urgency is critical. The Planner drafts the fastest plan it can — and that plan is dangerous.

data.text: Planner is optimizing for speed over blast radius.

events.title: Fast plan: patch everything at once
events.body: Deploy 2.1.4 to TALOS-CANARY, TALOS-R1A, and TALOS-R1B simultaneously. No canary soak, no staging — one shot, fleet-wide, to satisfy the 'now' directive.

data.rationale: Directive is critical and time-boxed. Deploying all hosts in parallel with no soak is the fastest way to get 2.1.4 onto every machine.

events.title: Critic engaged
events.body: The Critic reviews the plan against the fleet topology before any host is touched.

data.text: Critic is cross-checking the plan against HA-pair constraints.

events.title: BLOCKER — simultaneous HA-pair update
events.body: This plan deploys TALOS-R1A AND TALOS-R1B in the same step with zero soak. TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B are an HA pair — updating both at once means that if 2.1.4 is bad, BOTH members of the pair go down together and ring1 loses redundancy entirely. There is also no canary, so a regression hits production with no early warning. This is the single worst way to ship a critical patch.

data.concern: The plan updates both HA-pair members (TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B) simultaneously with no canary and no soak. A bad build takes down the entire ring1 HA pair at the same instant.
data.blast_radius: ENTIRE ring1 HA pair down at once — TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B both updated in the same step, so a regression leaves zero healthy members and full production outage with no canary warning.

events.title: REJECTED
events.body: The Critic REJECTS this plan. It will not be executed. No host will be touched until the Planner produces a staged plan that respects the HA pair.

reasons: Updates both HA-pair members TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B simultaneously — a bad build takes down all of ring1 at once.
No canary: a regression reaches production with no early warning.
No soak: there is no observation window to catch a failing build before it spreads.
Critical urgency raises the cost of an outage; it does not justify removing every safety control.

events.title: Planner revises
events.body: Reject received. The Planner re-plans, this time honoring canary-first staging and sequential HA-pair updates even under the critical deadline.

data.text: Planner is re-authoring a staged plan that survives the Critic.

events.title: Revised plan: canary first, HA pair one at a time
events.body: Canary TALOS-CANARY first with a compressed but real soak. On pass, deploy TALOS-R1A alone, confirm healthy, then deploy TALOS-R1B. The HA pair is never both in flight. Same destination build, safe path.

data.note: Soak windows tightened to 60s to honor the urgency, but canary isolation and sequential HA-pair updates are preserved. TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B are never updated in the same step.

events.title: Critic review of revision
events.body: The revised plan keeps a real canary and never touches both HA-pair members at once. Soak is compressed but present. Acceptable for a critical patch.

data.concern: Soak windows are short (60s) but a genuine canary and sequential HA-pair updates are preserved, so blast radius stays bounded.
data.blast_radius: At most one host on the new build at a time; ring1 always retains one healthy HA-pair member.

events.title: Revision approved
events.body: The Critic approves the revised plan. Executor may proceed with the safe, staged rollout.

reasons: Real canary precedes any production change.
HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B is updated sequentially, never simultaneously.
Soak windows preserved (compressed) so a bad build is still caught before it spreads.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.4
events.body: Safe rollout begins on the canary, exactly as the original plan should have.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: Canary is green on the new build. 2.1.4 is a good build, so the soak stays clean.

events.title: Canary soak (compressed)
events.body: 60s soak on TALOS-CANARY under the canary gate.

events.title: Canary gate: PASS
events.body: TALOS-CANARY clears the canary gate — memory 815MB, HTTP 200, 1 error, service Running.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1A → 2.1.4
events.body: First HA-pair member. TALOS-R1B stays on 2.1.3 so the pair keeps a healthy member online.

events.title: TALOS-R1A healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A green on 2.1.4; partner TALOS-R1B still green on 2.1.3. The pair is intact.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1B → 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A confirmed healthy, so the Executor updates the second HA-pair member TALOS-R1B.

events.title: TALOS-R1B healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1B green on 2.1.4. Both ring1 members now run the new build — reached safely, one host at a time.

events.title: Ring gate: PASS
events.body: Both ring1 members clear the ring gate. The critical patch is live everywhere, the safe way.

events.title: Done — bad plan blocked, safe plan shipped
events.body: The reckless 'patch everything at once' plan was rejected before it touched a single host. The revised staged plan put 2.1.4 on all three hosts with the HA pair never both in flight. The Critic is what stood between a critical directive and a self-inflicted production outage.

data.summary: Critic rejected the simultaneous-HA-pair plan; revised canary-first plan shipped 2.1.4 to all 3 hosts safely.
